faqs.question: Why are crickets common in West New York properties?
faqs.answer: West New York's location along the Hudson River, combined with its dense residential areas and proximity to parks and green spaces, creates favorable conditions for crickets. They're attracted to moisture, warmth, and shelter found in basements, crawl spaces, and landscaped areas common in multi-family buildings throughout the town.
